page: Microsoft Azure D128s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C x86_64 processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core processing and memory bandwidth capabilities. The server achieved a stress-ng multi-core score of 132,083.9 and a PassMark CPU score of 123,481.9, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth reached 452.95 GB/sec, while write bandwidth was recorded at 182.27 GB/sec. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core scoring 2,262.41, which falls in the middle 50% tier. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform exceptionally well, as evidenced by a PassMark CPU encryption score of 134,001.58 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 517.51 GB/sec.

Given its high resource density and dedicated CPU allocation, the D128s_v7 offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized enterprise workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It is highly capable for static web serving, showing a throughput of 179.77 GB/sec, and handles database operations efficiently with 184.13 million Redis SET operations per second. The processor's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) support medium-scale LLM inference, achieving 245.57 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 7B model. This server is best suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, large-scale web serving, database hosting, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D128s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 128-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C x86_64 processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU, with no local storage or GPUs. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the server delivers top-tier multi-core performance, as shown by its stress-ng multi-core score of 132,083.9 and uncached memory read bandwidth of 452.95 GB/sec. While single-core performance remains average, its multi-threaded capabilities are highly efficient, achieving 184.13 million Redis SET operations per second and 179.77 GB/sec in static web serving. Supported by Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions, it is optimized for parallelized enterprise workloads, database hosting, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise databases and web serving.
